_about: Single source of truth for every plot in the deck. index.html (Chart.js) and build_plots.py (matplotlib) both read this file so the live charts and the PNG backups show identical numbers. All headline numbers are the CORRECTED 29-query public set unless a series says otherwise.

recall_at_depth._source: docs/DIAGNOSIS.md 'Core measurements' / experiments/diagnose_retrieval.py (29-query corrected set, PURE BM25, NDCG@10=0.3191)
recall_at_depth.title: Recall is fine — ranking is the bottleneck
recall_at_depth.annotation: 100% retrievable by rank 500, but only 66% land in the top 10

progression._source: docs/DIAGNOSIS.md 'The winning recipe' + README results table (29-query corrected set)
progression.title: Each component earns its place

beta_sweep._source: experiments/diagnose_hybrid.py tables in docs/DIAGNOSIS.md (over the BM25 candidate pool, 29-query set)
beta_sweep.title: BM25 fusion lets the length prior go further
beta_sweep.annotation: Prior alone collapses past beta=0.05; fused with BM25 it tolerates beta=0.15

grid._source: docs/DIAGNOSIS.md (dense_w x beta) grid + experiments/diagnose_sweep.py / PROGRESS.md L4 (29-query set, cand_n=300)
grid.title: (dense_w x beta) — our operating point is the grid maximum

ablation._source: docs/DIAGNOSIS.md 'Refuted ideas' + lever table (logs/eval-public-*-1781593521.log, 29-query set). NOTE: body-chunk 0.4115 is the CORRECTED-set re-test, not the old corrupted-set 0.10-0.12.
ablation.title: What we tried that did not beat 0.4338

cand_n._source: experiments/diagnose_sweep.py / PROGRESS.md L1 (29-query set, dense_w=0.8, beta=0.15)
cand_n.title: Widening the candidate pool only hurts

length_contrast._source: docs/DIAGNOSIS.md 'Dataset shape' — summary stats only (no full distribution committed); illustrative two-bar comparison
length_contrast.title: Answers are short; distractors are long
length_contrast.note: Answer pages ~50-280 words (midpoint shown); distractor median ~1218 words
